settings.about: The Sri Krishna Sanatan Seva Federation (SKSSF) is a dedicated non-political youth-led organization established to preserve, promote, and propagate the timeless values of Sanatan Dharma. We conduct community services, organize holy festivals, preserve historical temples, and cultivate youth leadership grounded in service, peace, and spiritual harmony.
settings.mission: To empower Sanatana youth with Vedic wisdom, engage in humanitarian relief, maintain ancient architectural heritage, and build an inclusive, spiritually conscious community.
settings.vision: A vibrant, globally connected Sanatana community where truth (Satya), righteousness (Dharma), and service (Seva) inspire holistic progress and peaceful coexistence.
settings.address: Block-B, Sector 4, Banasree, Rampura, Dhaka-1219, Bangladesh
settings.footerText: © 2026 Sri Krishna Sanatan Seva Federation. All Rights Reseved. Developed by AI Studio Portal.

notices.title: Notice regarding Sri Sri Radhashtami festival preparations and Volunteer formation
notices.content: All registered spiritual members of the federation are cordially requested to join the upcoming coordination meeting on Saturday, 6th June 2026, at 4:30 PM in the Head Office mandir hall. We will establish the core festival subcommittee, recruit local volunteers, select coordinators, and plan prasadam distribution routes. Please bring your official SKSSF PVC Identity Cards for verification.

notices.title: Mandatory PVC Identity Card Download and Verification Link
notices.content: This is to inform all members that the automated PVC Identification Card system is now live. Please log in using the credentials given to you at the time of your application approval. You can view, download, or directly print your standard 300 DPI high-resolution CRM PVC ID card. The QR code on the front and back of the plastic card is fully verifiable at any event gate.

notices.title: Weekly Gita Study & Spiritual Dialogue Session Notice
notices.content: By the grace of Sri Bhagavan, our weekly Bhagavad Gita recitation, sloka pronunciation, and theological discussion panel sessions occur every Friday afternoon from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM. High-quality booklets will be provided free of charge to all children and youth attendees. Pranam, all are requested to participate with family.

events.title: Holy Janmashtami Maha-Utsav and Nagar Kirtan Parade 2026
events.description: Celebrating the glorious appearance of Sri Lord Krishna with grand dynamic tableaus, Vedic dance presentations, extensive Nagar Kirtan parade, spiritual discussions, cultural quizzes for youths, and distributing divine Mahaprasadam to 10,000+ devotees. All members are urged to select a volunteer shift.
events.location: Dhakeshwari National Temple Complex & Dhaka Streets

events.title: Maha Shivaratri Jagaran and Harinam Sankirtan Festival 2026
events.description: Full night of spiritual vigils, singing divine hymns, Vedic Rudrabhishek recitations, and holy bathing services under senior monks. We hosted thousands of night devotees safely. Extensive sanitary camps were handled entirely by our registered youth volunteers.
events.location: Chandra Nath Dham Temple Hill, Sitakunda, Chattogram

events.title: Sanatan Youth Leadership Convention & Spiritual Seminar 2026
events.description: A convergence of Hindu youths, academics, and spiritual pioneers focusing on mental health, community defense, Vedic educational integration, and building charity foundations. Featuring modern bento presentations, brainstorming segments, and expert talks.

customPages.title: The Ten Pillars of Sanatana Dharma
customPages.html:
Vedic Code of Life

Sanatana Dharma defines a set of eternal ethical values that guide human actions towards ultimate enlightenment. These are described as the ten pillars of righteousness:

  1. Dhriti (Patience and determination)
  2. Kshama (Forgiveness and tolerance)
  3. Dama (Self-control and mental discipline)
  4. Asteya (Non-stealing and truthfulness in transactions)
  5. Shaucham (Purity of body, mind, and intent)
  6. Indriyanigraha (Sensory regulation)
  7. Dhi (Intellect and discriminative wisdom)
  8. Vidya (True sacred knowledge)
  9. Satya (Absolute truthfulness in speech and deed)
  10. Akrodha (Freedom from unnecessary anger)
